method: explicit session authorization - owner delegated the trigger to the assistant
authorization: publicala y dejamela instalada en mi pc | Avanza con lo que es la compilacion y los carteles de error en todos los idiomas para el minero, y subilo. Como 1.1.0 todas mis bendiciones y autorizaciones.
scope: 1.1.0 adds pt-BR, zh-CN and ru (five languages), first-run OS-language detection, a five-language picker, one shared rule for which languages write decimals with a comma, and a Mine-view scroll fix. The launch notice announces 15:00 UTC only, with no local-time aside. It also rewrites two error messages: a failed connection to the app's own node now names the likely cause (antivirus) instead of falling through to a generic failure, and the missing-component message blames quarantine rather than the download. No changes to consensus, key derivation, mining or security.
version_reuse_note: The owner was offered 1.1.1 for the error-message rebuild and chose to keep 1.1.0, twice and explicitly. The trade-off was stated to him: anyone already on 1.1.0 will not be offered this fix, and two different binaries now share one version number. He accepted it on the grounds that the user base is still small and the fix is not critical. The earlier v1.1.0 release and tag were removed, so only these bytes carry the name.
